name: White Chocolate Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease and flour an 8-inch square baking pan.
2. Melt the butter and 4 ounces of white chocolate together in the top of a double boiler over hot water. Once melted, remove from heat and add the remaining white chocolate. Stir until blended well. Set aside to cool slightly.
3. Beat the eggs. Add the sugar and beat for 2-3 minutes until thickened. Gradually add the white chocolate and butter mixture, vanilla extract, and flour, beating just until smooth. Mix in the chocolate chunks using a spatula or your hands—do not overmix.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool on a wire rack before cutting into squares or bars.
